soap:
RE-REVIEW · lancedb/lancedb#3567 — fix(python): raise clear ValueError when vector column cannot be infe…
reviewed 777d2dc1 → 25f8c656  (1 commit, 1 file in delta)

CONDITIONS LEDGER (1/1 met)

  ✓ met      [blocking] Add a unit test for the fix (wjones127, CHANGES_REQUESTED @ 777d2dc1)
             → python/python/tests/test_util.py:853 — new test_infer_vector_column_raises_clear_error added in the delta; asserts pytest.raises(ValueError, match="vector") on table.search([1.0, 2.0]).to_list(). Condition satisfied as literally stated (a test now exists). See caveat in fresh-eyes — the test passes, but not for the reason its docstring claims.

FRESH-EYES DELTA

  issue(blocking): The new regression test is green but does not exercise the new guard, and its docstring documents a mechanism that is false.
  → The test's premise ("inf_vector_column_query silently returned None which then caused a confusing TypeError") is incorrect. In the head tree, util.py inf_vector_column_query is typed -> str and already raises ValueError("There is no vector column in the data...") when zero vector columns are found. It never returns None. So table.search([1.0, 2.0]) (query is not None) takes the inf_vector_column_query branch at util.py:308–310 and raises there — the test's match="vector" matches that pre-existing message, NOT the new guard's "No vector column found in the schema..." message at util.py:312–317. The test would have passed before this PR's util.py change.

  issue(blocking): The new `if vector_column_name is None:` guard (util.py:312–317) is effectively dead for the no-vector-column path it claims to fix. Because inf_vector_column_query raises first, the guard can only fire when query is None and query_type not in ("fts","hybrid") — a different path the new test does not cover. This directly substantiates wjones127's still-open inline question(blocking) at util.py:310. The removal of the try/except (which only re-raised) is harmless, but the added guard does not deliver the behavior the PR title/test advertise.

  Note: the only file in the delta since last review is the test. The util.py change landed in the base commit (777d2dc1) that was reviewed; it is re-surfaced here because the head-anchored blocking question targets it.

RECOMMENDATION: comment (do not block on the test-existence condition; do hold on the unresolved correctness question)
  The lone stated condition (add a test) is met. But the fresh-eyes pass shows the test is a tautology that doesn't cover the new code path, and the new guard doesn't fix the advertised case — wjones127's blocking question remains unanswered and is correct. Ask the author to (a) fix the test docstring, (b) add a case that actually reaches the new guard (query=None path), or confirm the guard is removed in favor of the existing inf_vector_column_query raise.
  confidence: high (verified against head-tree source of both functions) · what would make this wrong: if a caller of infer_vector_column_name passes query=None for plain vector search such that the new guard is the real hot path — worth a 2-line check of the search() → infer_vector_column_name call site.
